subject: history
question: Who was the first Prime Minister of India?
explanation: Nehru served as PM from 1947 to 1964 — the longest-serving Indian PM.

subject: history
question: In which year did India gain independence?
explanation: India became independent on 15 August 1947 after nearly 200 years of British rule.

subject: history
question: Who is called the Father of the Nation in India?
explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led India's independence movement through non-violent civil disobedience.

subject: history
question: Who built the Taj Mahal?
explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal (1632-1653) in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

subject: history
question: When did World War I begin?
explanation: WWI began on 28 July 1914, triggered by the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand.

subject: history
question: The Indian National Congress was founded in which year?
explanation: The INC was founded on 28 December 1885 in Bombay by A.O. Hume.

subject: history
question: Who was the first President of independent India?
explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad served as the first President of India from 1950 to 1962.

subject: history
question: Columbus discovered America in which year?
explanation: Columbus reached the Americas on 12 October 1492, while searching for a route to Asia.

subject: history
question: The Dandi March was led by which leader?
explanation: Gandhi led the 389-km Dandi March in 1930 to protest British salt taxes.

subject: history
question: In which year was the Constitution of India adopted?
explanation: The Constitution was adopted on 26 November 1949 and came into force on 26 January 1950.

subject: history
question: Who was the first woman Prime Minister of India?
explanation: Indira Gandhi became PM in 1966 and served until 1977, then again from 1980 until her death in 1984.

subject: history
question: The Ashoka Chakra on India's flag has how many spokes?
explanation: The 24 spokes of the Dharma Chakra represent the 24 hours of the day, symbolising progress.

subject: history
question: When did the Titanic sink?
explanation: RMS Titanic sank on 15 April 1912 after hitting an iceberg in the North Atlantic.

subject: history
question: Who designed the Indian National Flag?
explanation: Pingali Venkayya designed the flag in 1921; it was officially adopted on 22 July 1947.

subject: history
question: The Quit India Movement was launched in which year?
explanation: Gandhi launched 'Quit India' on 8 August 1942, demanding an end to British rule.

subject: history
question: In which year did India conduct its first nuclear test?
explanation: India's Pokhran-I test (Operation Smiling Buddha) took place on 18 May 1974.

subject: history
question: The first moon landing was in which year?
explanation: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon on 20 July 1969. Neil Armstrong was first to step on it.

subject: history
question: Who built the Red Fort in Delhi?
explanation: Shah Jahan built the Lal Qila (Red Fort) in 1638-48 as his new capital Shahjahanabad's centrepiece.

subject: history
question: Who was the founder of the Sikh religion?
explanation: Guru Nanak Dev (1469-1539) founded Sikhism and is revered as the first of ten Sikh Gurus.

subject: history
question: Which dynasty built the Qutub Minar?
explanation: Qutub-ud-din Aibak of the Slave Dynasty started construction in 1193 CE.

subject: history
question: Who wrote 'Discovery of India'?
explanation: Nehru wrote this book in 1945 while imprisoned in Ahmadnagar Fort.

subject: history
question: In which year did the Berlin Wall fall?
explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on 9 November 1989, leading to German reunification in 1990.

subject: history
question: Who was the last Mughal Emperor?
explanation: Bahadur Shah Zafar was the last Mughal Emperor, exiled to Rangoon by the British after 1857.

subject: history
question: The Sepoy Mutiny of 1857 is also called?
explanation: Nationalists call it India's First War of Independence; it started at Meerut on 10 May 1857.

subject: history
question: Who led the Maurya Empire after Ashoka's conversion to Buddhism?
explanation: Ashoka continued ruling after adopting Buddhism in ~260 BCE, spreading Dhamma across Asia.

subject: history
question: When did the French Revolution begin?
explanation: The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille on 14 July.

subject: history
question: The Battle of Panipat was first fought in which year?
explanation: The First Battle of Panipat (1526) saw Babur defeat Ibrahim Lodi, establishing the Mughal Empire.

subject: history
question: Who was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize?
explanation: Marie Curie won Nobel Prizes in Physics (1903) and Chemistry (1911) — the only person to win in two sciences.

subject: history
question: Chandragupta Maurya founded which empire?
explanation: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Maurya Empire around 322 BCE, unifying most of India.

subject: history
question: Which king fought against Alexander the Great in India?
explanation: King Porus (Paurava) fought bravely at the Battle of Hydaspes in 326 BCE. Alexander was impressed.

subject: history
question: The Amritsar Massacre (Jallianwala Bagh) happened in which year?
explanation: On 13 April 1919, General Dyer ordered soldiers to fire on unarmed civilians; ~400 were killed.

subject: history
question: Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed on the Moon in which year?
explanation: Chandrayaan-3 landed near the lunar south pole on 23 August 2023, making India the 4th nation to land on Moon.

subject: history
question: What was Gandhi's strategy of non-violent resistance called?
explanation: Satyagraha ('truth-force') was Gandhi's philosophy of non-violent resistance to injustice.
